content:
===accordion ===panel: Full Disclosure
===center **DO NOT BE FOOLED BY THIS DECKS STRANGE BUILD** ===endcenter While traditionally [[Rocco,Street Chef]] is tuned/built to be a more storm centric commander -- this deck seeks utilize them as more of a value card draw engine. Inspired by Surreal's original Rocco decklist, I have been playing and tuning this list for the past couple of years and it has been one of if not my favourite casual decklist. [[Rocco, Street Chef]] allows you to play the best cards in three incredibly powerful strategies at once (Exile matters, Tokens, +1/+1 Counters) and acts as the glue to amplify these synergies. Pivoting between each strategy as most cards fullfill multiple, you'll find the card quality derived from the decks synergy to be much higher than it looks to be on paper. You eventually bury your opponents in value and pressure as Rocco is strong enough to storm off even without dedicating the deck to it. Instead we focus on high value creatures such as [[Basking Broodscale]], [[Dusk Legion Duelist]] , [[Generous Patron]] to create several hard to remove mini engines to control the board and pace of the game. This deck when played correctly never runs out of options and is able to hang through all phases of the game. Some Rule 0 stipulations for the deck - no infinites, no tutors, no combos. ===center **Bring this list to your local playgroup at your own discretion!** **Surreal Rocco Decklist** https://www.moxfield.com/decks/SsvBcfKNaU-S0nc-PEH7jg **$200 Budget Version** https://moxfield.com/decks/qyMPDn4HIk2D33VFVvWoVA ===endcenter ===center ===endcenter ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Pros + Cons
===center #### ✅ **PROS** ✅ ===endcenter ===center **If any of this sounds fun to the gameplay experience you are looking for you'll enjoy this deck** ===endcenter - **Consistency**: Consistent early gameplan to establish small value creatures and [[Rocco, Street Chef]] to prevent non-games while also consistently creating unique gameplay experiences with the RNG factor of our commander's abillity * **Lot's of synergy & cool interactions**: This deck is filled with so many unique interactions that you don't really see elsewhere (synergies further explained below) that each game feels like a fun puzzle solve. You often find a single [[Rocco,Street Chef]] trigger enabling several other game actions to further your value pile. - **Resilient**: While having [[Rocco, Street Chef]] in play is a key part to snowballing games, it’s not essential. The deck by design focuses on a midrange plan by having stand alone cards such as [[Anim Pakal, Thousandth Moon]] + [[Baylen, the Haymaker]] + [[Dragonhawk, Fate's Tempest]] etc that maintain board pressure/card value without Rocco in play (but are further strengthened with them in play) This makes the deck incredibly resilient in being able to not only pivot but makes the gameplan incredibly hard to shutdown. We have alot of strong midrange engines and top end within the deck that allow us to pivot to other game plans if our commander is shut down early. ===center #### ⛔ **CONS** ⛔ ===endcenter ===center **If any of this sounds like it doesn't meet your goals for your commander gameplay experience this deck may not be for you** ===endcenter - **Your playgroup might hate you**: This deck is incredibly powerful and melds the storm archetype with the battlecruiser archetype making is a particularly hard matchup for many other casual decks. Your deck has better and more threats than other battlecruiser decks while also being faster and more threatening to spellslinger decks that aren't able to protect themselves. The engine that your commander creates is so powerful that even after a wipe you'll most likely be able to rebuild faster than anybody else at the table. * **You'll need alot of tokens and dice**: Be sure to bring lots of tokens to denote items in this deck. You will be taking alot of game actions towards the mid/late game and your playgroup might hate you for it. Requires alot of tracking of triggers (such as counters, tokens being made, etc). ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Deck Philosophy + General Gameplan
===center #### **INTRODUCTION** ===endcenter This deck was initially the love child of a good friend (Surreal) as I personally really struggled with making [[Rocco,Street Chef]] not only viable to different pod matchups but also fun. From other lists I had seen they were all very storm orientated or very all in on the commander - so much so that even with their variance, games would feel very much the same. You either did the thing and stormed off or your opponents would remove [[Rocco,Street Chef]]/play a singular stax pieces and end your game. In all honesty the deck is not a good storm deck but that is the popular/most common way to play it. Rather this one focuses on using [[Rocco, Street Chef]] as an engine starter while focusing on cards that are relevant both with and without our commander. This gives the deck a more resilient nature while also giving us a heavier focus on maximizing our synergies to break parity on [[Rocco,Street Chef]] ability. Yes the deck does have an ultimate inevitability as with most decks to ultimately overwhelm your opponents with value, but the deck has the flexibility of pushing for combat damage or burn damage through mixing Exile, +1/+1 Counters, and Token synergies.The deck, through countless amounts of games and experience I have found benefits the most from relying on Token synergies then Exile synergies and then finally Counter synergies, resulting in the composition of card choices built around this ideology. ===center #### **GENERAL GAMEPLAN** ===endcenter When approaching the game you generally want to look for a hand with a ramp to cast [[Rocco,Street Chef]] as well as have enough mana on the following turn to cast whatever you exile -- this often means looking for hands with ramp like [[Delighted Halfling]] which allows for a turn 2 commander play or [[incubation druid]] to allow for a powerful turn 4. Because our commander is so low to the ground generally it's easy to keep any hand with a turn 1-2 play into [[Rocco,Street Chef]] -- we have a dense amount of good engine starters such as [[Party Thrasher]] that can really launch our board from turn 3 onwards. From then on it's just about establishing the rest of your value engine and pivoting between which strategies work best for the current pod. Given how many game actions you take, many players can find [[Rocco,Street Chef]] to be very scary even without a payoff, I usually sandbag other value creatures like [[Aerial Extortionist]] or [[Urabrask, Heretic Praetor]] if I already have a decent engine of mana and card draw going so that when the wipe happens I can run out these cards to start rebuilding faster than my opponents (you'll notice most of our 5+ mana cards are huge engines/payoffs all on their own that get amplified by our commander). The mid-late game is generally just keeping up the pressure on opponents life total while outvaluing them (there really isn't a correct option to play against our commander, choosing not to play the card just means we're exiling potential gas from our opponents library while we will consistently be up the one card and choosing to play the card will trigger our synergies too many times for them to compete with). At a certain point it just feels like your'e dropping draft bomb after draft bomb until your opponents have no way to remove the endless amount of value you garner. With our value engine we are trying to pressure our opponents boards and remove key pieces off their board -- you'll notice we run a large suite of one sided removal in [[Volanic Torrent]] + [[Delayed Blast Fireball]] + [[Call Forth the Tempest]] -- all which has great synergy with our commander. Our creatures usually get big enough to strike our opponents life totals down and for anyone who's board is too big we can pivot to a burn plan with [[Dragonhawk, Fate's Tempest]] + [[Weftstalker Ardent]] + [[Quintorious Kand]] etc. You'll find yourself eventually storming off on your own when you get to the late game with things like [[Jaheira, Friend of the Forest]] making you so much mana that even without dedicating the deck fully to storm, if [[Rocco,Street Chef]] is left unchecked you can easily start spell slinging to burn your opponents out or smashing in for huge damage. You'll find value engines such as [[Generous Patron]] or the [[Bennie Bracks]] effects really start to help you churn through your deck. Not only do they replace themselves almost immediately they become single card draw engines with your commander in play making them incredibly high impact for low investment. Alot of the cards in the list are similiar to one card starter and engines in tandem with Rocco (but can also exist without Rocco - ie. [[Aerial Extortionist]] ) - and we play minor elements of stax to keep disrupting our opponents from being able to get a foot hold -- cards like [[Uba Mask]] + [[Urabrask, Heretic Praetor]] work to disrupt our opponents in a way that is minor enough for us to not draw hate from the entire table. ===center ## **[[symbol:r]][[symbol:g]][[symbol:w]]Decklist All-Stars[[symbol:r]][[symbol:g]][[symbol:w]]** ===endcenter
===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel:Notable Inclusions
* **[[Devoted Druid]]** : A card I was initially hesitant on but convinced to test by a friend (Noobzaurs) this card may scare some folks as a combo piece (just make sure you tell them there is not combo in the deck), this has actually turned out to be one of the best dorks in the list. The ability to tap asynchronously by removing counters makes [[Devoted Druid]] unlike [[Kami of Whispered Hopes]] where you may HAVE to use the dork mana to cast a spell out of exile and as a result turn off your +1/+1 Counter payoff - whereas [[Devoted Druid]] can receive the counter and untap herself to allow you to keep chaining spells as you are trying to advance your board. Pretty freakin busted. - **[[Caretaker's Talent]]** : Essentially another copy of [[Bennie Bracks, Zoologist]] in practice, [[Caretaker's Talent]] has proven to have a ton more utility and synergy with the deck. Even copying the extra token comes in very clutch with different cards in the list (for example copying a food token to trigger [[Sentinel Sarah Lyons]] pump effect or even a thopter token from [[Pia Nalaar, Consul of Revival]] to get an extra [[Kutzil, Malamet Exemplar]] trigger). The final ability on [[Caretaker's Talent]] also synergizes incredibly well with all of our token makers and often gives the extra push needed to end games. Just a ton of utillity on a card draw engine that is namely not a creature so it's not at risk of dying to board wipes and a bit harder to remove. ===center **CARDS CURRENTLY BEING TESTED** ===endcenter * **[[Collector's Cage]]** - a card I initially overlooked when constructing and testing the deck but was brought to my attention after seeing it perform in 1v1. I've ran multiple games with the card over the last month and have been pleasantly suprised with just how efficient the card is. The hideway 5 lets you dig pretty deep for just 2 mana and the cost to cast the hideaway card is essentially free given the amount of creatures we run. Often I read it as three mana cast the best card from the top of your library (5 deep) for free because that's how the play pattern has been. On top of that, it leaves behind a artifact that has synergy with [[Inspiring Statuary]] + [[Sentinel Sarah Lyons]] + [[Ghirapur Aether Grid]] etc, that allows you to put a counter per cycle on a creature to trigger any incidental counter synergies as well. The floor on the card is very high and so is the ceiling (even if you don't find anything great in the top 5, it often ends up clearing 5 bad poential draws with the upside of being an artifact that can trigger counter synergies, or at the high end it can flip to reveal a massive bomb to cheat out). It's just a pretty crazy card for 2 mana. - **[[Whiskervale Forerunner]]** : A bit of another unorthodox card that isn't seeing much play in most lists - I currently run 25 hits (which based on wizards R&D could increase in the future) but this is another card that is just a mini-engine with our commander. Allowing us to cheat out value pieces as well as fill our hand to further push the board/keep up value engines to sandbag - this is honestly a big overperformer. It's easy to cast with only being one white pip and has a big body to carry counters. * **[[Kirol, Attentive First-Year]]** : Another great include into the list that similiar to [[Whiskervale Forerunner]] I overlooked initially. Our deck is heavily creature based so the restriction of tapping creatures to double a trigger is not a chokepoint whatsoever, Rocco + Kirol alone allow you to double Rocco's end step trigger to exile an extra card for everyone or to make extra food tokens/counters. What makes Kirol more intriguing as a doubler is the insane range of doubling it offers when compared to something like [[Roaming Throne]]. It also isn't nearly as scary as the aforementioned card when sat on the battlefield and also offers utillity when our commander is not in play. In total we have an upwards total of 30+ possible cards that can be doubled with Kirol. Potentially the most broken part of Kirol's abillity is the way it interacts with once per turn triggers differently than something like a [[Delney, Streetwise Lookout]]. Cards such as [[Dusk Legion duelist]] which have the clause "once per turn" traditionally cannot be doubled up on when paired with Roaming Throne or Delney. Kirol on the other hand is able to copy the abillity on the stack which circumvents the once per turn clause. It allows cards such as; [[Bennie Bracks, Zoologist]] [[Terrasymbiosis]] [[Caretaker's Talent]] to double trigger and net you a second draw off of a single trigger. On top of that, it also doubles up ETB triggers such as [[Etali, Primal conqueror]] and even [[Showdown of the Skalds]] which off insane amounts of value. This card has such wide ranging utility that it would be silly to not consider it. ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Notable Exclusions
- **[[Laelia, the Blade Reforged]]** : The second closest card to KCI that could see it's way into the deck -- [[Laelia, the Blade Reforged]] is a generically strong creature that provides consistent card draw/a [[Rocco, Street Chef]] trigger. [[Laelia, the Blade Reforged]] can get really big with the number of cards we exile but the main reason I don't run it for the time being is it's weakness in the later stages of the game. It only really enables/synergizes with the exile portion of our deck but when compared to something like [[Anim, Pakal Thousandth Moon]] which has synergies with both the counters and artifact token portion of the deck -- it's just a less consistently powerful card than what we already have. Without our commander in play - Anim can still run amok and set up the board for our other synergies while Laelia just becomes an inconsistent card draw engine. * **[[Ingenious Artillerist]]** : Another copy of [[Reckless Fireweaver]] that offers the potential for a huge amount of damage. I cut this as it didn't necessarily feel needed and at three mana being a 3/1 made it incredibly vulnerable to removal. I found myself instead finding other avenues of damage in [[Quintorious Kand]], [[Dragonhawk, Fate's Tempest]], [[Ghirapur Aethergrid]] and haven't felt like I've missed the second Fireweaver since. - **[[Doubling Season]]** : The ultimate casual trap card for this list in my opinion - it comes down and does nothing on it's own and the overall status/reputation of the card instantly makes you and your board seem way more terrifying than it actually may be. While no doubt doubling the food tokens and counters is powerful, in practice this card disincentivizes opponents from playing their cards from exile more than any other card and even when considering our specific synergies in the list such as [[Dusk Legion Duelist]], the extra counters don't matter (what's more important is the instances of counters ie. [[Basking Broodscale]]). If that's the case it just becomes a less effecient and more expensive token doubler where in this case, 5 mana is definitely not worth it. * **[[Pia Nalaar, Consul of Revival]]** + - **[[Faldorn, Dread Wolf Herald]]** : Probably the two cuts I'm making that will get me the most cancelled. These just feel a bit too low impact for what the deck is trying to do. Pia conflicts with our play pattern of trying to go into a turn 2 [[Rocco, Street Chef]] or ramping on turn 2 for a big turn 4 play with our commander in play. Yes they are enhancers to Rocco and also function without him but there are other cards fill similar functions. Exiling Pia or Faldorn as one of your first exile passes makes for an incredibly weak play - going into what is essentially another piece (Rocco and Pia) that requires exiling to generate the pay off of....making a 1/1 thopter or a 2/2 wolf? For the amount of card advantage and velocity this deck turns out wants much higher impact cards and for exile "enhancers" we are basically looking towards cards like [[Roaming Throne]] which double all avenues of synergy. ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Mulligan Guide
===center ### **Hand 1** ===endcenter
===center A great example of an strong hand I'd be looking for when playing this list. We get a clean surveil on turn one into ramping on turn 2 with our [[Arcane Signet]] and allow us to cast possible 4 mana value spells off of [[Rocco,Street Chef]]. We also have continued value engines/pingers in [[Quintorious Kand]] and [[Reckless Fireweaver]] to further our gameplan and pressure life totals. This is a hand that I would keep in almost any situation - in the case of going down to a six I would considering bottoming [[Inspiring Statuary]] or a land if I'm feeling greedy (or in first seat). ===endcenter ===center ### **Hand 2** ===endcenter
===center While this hand includes the ability to cast our commander on turn 2, it is a bit more of a risky keep since our only piece of value comes in [[Annie Joins up]] which is a nice bit of removal but forces us onto the [[Rocco, Street Chef]] gameplan as our only way to play the game. It marks our commander as a huge target from the jump and without something like an [[Urabrask, Heretic Praetor]] that can be it's own standalone engine without our commander, we run the risk of jamming our commander early and getting blown out with no real way to rebuild. Going down to six I would bin [[Bala Ged Recovery]] from this hand. ===endcenter ===center ### **Hand 3** ===endcenter
===center A below average hand but not completely unkeepable. On a first seven I would definitely mulligan this hand but funnily enough this is a great hand when going down to six or even five (bottoming [[Ruric Thar, the Unbowed]] and [[Turntimber Symbiosis]]). We can turn 1 fetch for a surveil to smooth out our draws and play a slower game plan for [[Rocco,Street Chef]] on curve on turn 3 to set up either a [[Jeska's will]] or [[Showdown of the Skalds]] to hopefully refuel us/get us back into the game. This hand's risk comes in that it really relies on our commander helping us to fix the hand for a smooth early game. Possible low floor but really high ceiling. ===endcenter ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Bling Showcase
===center ## **SUMOMO CARDS ALTER**
===endpanel ===endaccordion ===center ## HAPPY COOKING ===endcenter
